compiled_state.title: [design-review/review-criteria.md]: caller_id omission has no skill-level fallback -> validation skipped silently
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
Source: code review (passed) — minor finding deferred from compress-not-remove refactor.
Finding: The validation block in plugins/dso/skills/design-review/docs/review-criteria.md was compressed to 'Do not run validation a second time from this skill' on the basis that REVIEW-PROTOCOL-WORKFLOW.md owns invocation. If a future caller omits caller_id, the protocol-level validation will not validate the design-review-specific schema. The skill-level block was a defense-in-depth fallback.
Acceptance criterion: caller_id is currently hardcoded in plugins/dso/skills/design-review/SKILL.md, so omission is not a realistic failure mode for this skill. If caller_id ever becomes dynamic, restore an explicit per-caller validation invocation.

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral. File plugins/dso/skills/design-review/docs/review-criteria.md is in skills/ dir — routes through LLM-behavioral path. Root cause: Validation section says 'Do not run validation a second time from this skill' without providing a fallback for when caller_id is missing. If a caller omits caller_id, the protocol-level validation in REVIEW-PROTOCOL-WORKFLOW.md never fires, and the skill has no warning path — gap is silently swallowed. Fix: add an explicit skill-level fallback warning block to the Validation section.
comments.author: Test
